You would think it is the most horrifying, difficult, agonizing thing they've ever been able to, they've ever been asked to do in their life, you know, let alone four newsletters, weekly faxes, white papers, special reports, on and on and on and on. You can't find anybody in our world and in most other worlds who has built a really significant coaching business or a really significant consulting business who is not a writer. Now I don't mean by that, you know, Hemingway, but I do mean certain things. It is necessary. It is the primary means of communication and bonding between you and your client is the written word. And everything from the little memos that control their behavior at the meeting to the newsletter, to the article, to the sales letter, you've got to be able to write. If you pay to have it all done for you and you get it done by somebody who is any good, they will have all the money because it's really the single most important, most valuable and most expensive part of all this business. It is infinitely easier, for example, for me to teach somebody how to run a coaching meeting than it is to get somebody to sustain clients over years because they have to be able to write. And so for some it is, I know a piece of news you didn't necessarily want to hear, but in the interest of completeness I give it to you. I also tell you that it is my profound belief anybody can get good at it. It's actually easier to get good at it than to get good at speaking because there's some things that you can do it in private. It doesn't actually require you to get up here and be vulnerable and emotionally naked. You can hide with your computer and turn out the product, but you've got to be good at it. And there's certain things about it that are like essential. You've got to be, well, I lost a whole thing here I wanted to do, but I'm on my writing page when. I'll just do it the hard way. You've got to be prolific. You've got to be able to turn out a lot of stuff. Now that's more a matter of how much stuff you've got to work from than it is anything else, but you've got to be able to turn out a lot of stuff. You've got to be able to write persuasively. You've got to be able to reinforce your reputation, your fact list, all of the things you want them to know all the time through your writing. So it is far from just an exercise of writing. It is almost an exercise of strategic thinking that then leads to writing. The writing actually is the short part of the process. Only takes a few hours, for example, on my end to churn out a newsletter, but it takes all month to plan what I'm going to churn it out about, in what order, and what I'm trying to accomplish. And then to make sure I've covered all the bases, I've covered my facts. That takes more time. The writing, not that hard. But it's about being persuasive, about being good at reinforcing what it is that you want people to know about you, and being able to deliver the same fundamental core competencies to people over a long period of time, hold their interest in them without having any new core competencies. So we all only got a handful, two handfuls, three handfuls of things to teach. Now we've got to find ways to tell new stories about them, different stories, put them in different contexts. And so there's a lot to this, but it is important that you get good at it. Some of you, I know, some of you are very good at it already. Some of you are. That's so good, but a lot better than you were a few years ago. Some of you, of course, I don't know at all. But it's a pretty significant part of success in this field and success in this business.
